question.text: An InvIT has publicly listed units and the financial year ended on March 31st. By when must the full valuation for the half-year ending September 30th be completed?

answer.text: Within 45 days of the end of the half year
Within 15 days of the end of the half year
Within two months of the end of the half year (by November 30th)
Within one month of the end of the half year (by October 31st)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For publicly listed InvIT units, valuation has to be conducted every 6 months and completed within one month of the end of each half year. So for the half year ending September 30th, valuation must be completed by October 31st. **The Trap:** Two months is the timeline for full valuation from the end of the financial year (March 31st). The half-yearly valuation has a tighter one-month deadline. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Annual valuation = within 2 months of FY end; Half-yearly valuation (for listed units) = within 1 month of half-year end. The shorter timeline for interim valuations reflects market need for timely information.
